title: CII Pushes Green Hydrogen Mandates with Incentives to Unlock Demand; Seeks Policy Clarity
content: The Confederation of Indian Industry (CII) has urged the government to introduce Green Hydrogen Mandates to stimulate demand and build a vibrant green economy. CII Director General Chandrajit Banerjee noted that after Indiaâ€™s record 49.12 GW non-fossil capacity addition in 2025, promoting green hydrogen is vital. The proposal targets key sectors like refining and fertilizers, suggesting blending mandates supported by cost-offset mechanisms such as carbon credits and viability gap funding to address high costs. CII also advocates for public procurement mandates of 10â€“15% for green materials in infrastructure and the creation of industrial clusters with shared infrastructure in states like Gujarat and Maharashtra. To drive exports, the industry body recommends bilateral agreements with nations like Germany and Japan, granting "deemed export" status to green derivatives, and providing transition support to shield exporters from global carbon taxes like the EUâ€™s CBAM.
author: Analysts Account
